wishlist_policy.memory_note: Project memory is disabled. This XML file is the persistent wishlist artifact for this conversation.
wishlist_policy.retention_rule: Never delete a wish. When abandoned or superseded, change its status to retired or superseded and add a dated history note explaining why.
wishlist_policy.inference_rule: Add reasonably inferred wishes when they clearly follow from the stated product direction.

wish.title: Full community platform
wish.description: Build a full Milovana alternative with a forum, script catalogue, script editor, tease engine, user accounts and synchronized progression.

history.event: Added from the initial architecture brainstorming request.

wish.title: Low-cost server architecture
wish.description: Keep central server load and bandwidth low enough for an inexpensive VPS; execute heavy runtime work on client devices where practical.

history.event: Added from the initial architecture brainstorming request.

wish.title: Platform-hosted published media with optional private local media
wish.description: Serve published package images, audio and video through platform-managed object storage/CDN so packages do not depend on author-controlled hotlinks. Allow private user-owned high-resolution media to remain local where appropriate.

history.event: Added from the initial architecture brainstorming request.
Refined after choosing platform-hosted published media while retaining optional private local media.

wish.title: Private peer-to-peer links
wish.description: Use peer-to-peer connections where they materially reduce server load and improve privacy, particularly for desktop-phone pairing and live sessions.

history.event: Added from the initial architecture brainstorming request.
WebRTC remains product intent only; implementation requires a concrete browser limitation or peer-to-peer use case and an accepted decision.

wish.title: Strong consensual device-control mode
wish.description: Provide an optional installed desktop companion capable of immersive fullscreen or kiosk-like punishment tasks, with visible state, user-enabled integration settings and a reliable emergency exit. Avoid granular per-function permissions.

history.event: Added from the initial architecture brainstorming request.
Removed unnecessary granular permissions; core engine APIs are standard and only genuine external boundaries require one-time enablement.

wish.title: Webcam task validation
wish.description: Use on-device camera analysis to validate movement, posture, repetitions, task completion and before/after visual changes.

history.event: Added from the initial architecture brainstorming request.

wish.title: LLM-enhanced script runtime
wish.description: Allow an LLM to make dialogue more realistic while the deterministic script remains authoritative; support both low-freedom variable filling and bounded higher-freedom scene narration.

history.event: Added from the initial architecture brainstorming request.

wish.title: LLM behaviour and image assessment
wish.description: Allow an LLM or vision model to classify interaction signals such as bratty or submissive behaviour and assess task evidence, with confidence-aware branching.

history.event: Added from the initial architecture brainstorming request.

wish.title: Local LLM providers
wish.description: Support local inference through at least Ollama and llama.cpp, behind a provider-neutral interface.

history.event: Added from the initial architecture brainstorming request.

wish.title: Tool-using LLM
wish.description: Expose typed tools to the LLM for rhythm, timers, camera checks, task modules, line writing, corner time, chastity integrations and toy actions; all calls pass through runtime validation.

history.event: Added from the initial architecture brainstorming request.

wish.title: Desktop and smartphone specialization
wish.description: Support desktop-only, phone-only and dual-device task variants instead of forcing one interface model onto every device.

history.event: Added from the initial architecture brainstorming request.

wish.title: Cross-device handoff
wish.description: Allow a running tease to move between desktop and smartphone and to combine sensors from one device with rendering or local LLM execution on another.

history.event: Added from the initial architecture brainstorming request.

wish.title: Smartphone sensor tasks
wish.description: Support camera, accelerometer, orientation, step-count and other available mobile sensor signals for task verification where device support permits.

history.event: Added from the initial architecture brainstorming request.

wish.title: Toy integration
wish.description: Integrate existing ecosystems where practical, especially Buttplug/Intiface, xToys-compatible workflows and Chaster APIs.

history.event: Added from the initial architecture brainstorming request.

wish.title: Deterministic compatibility engine
wish.description: Run legacy Tease AI-style scripts predictably, with versioned compatibility profiles, reproducible random seeds, resumable state and traceable branching.

history.event: Added from the initial architecture brainstorming request.

wish.title: Safe script publishing model
wish.description: Publish scripts as versioned packages rather than arbitrary executable code; include validation, simulation, dependency metadata and only the external integration requirements that actually cross browser, device, network or OS boundaries.

history.event: Added from the initial architecture brainstorming request.
Removed unnecessary granular permissions; core engine APIs are standard and only genuine external boundaries require one-time enablement.

wish.title: Client-owned private data
wish.description: Prefer local processing for camera, private media, LLM inference and detailed task evidence; synchronize only the minimum account and progression data required.

history.event: Added from the initial architecture brainstorming request.

wish.title: Cross-platform desktop client
wish.description: Provide an installed desktop client for Windows and Linux, with macOS support where practical, while sharing as much user-interface and engine code as possible.

history.event: Inferred from the technology-stack and platform-support requirements.
A full desktop client remains deferred; mobile starts as a responsive PWA and a thin helper is added only for concrete browser limitations.

wish.title: Server-independent local operation
wish.description: Allow the tease engine, local scripts, media, local LLM and device integrations to run without a live server connection; the server acts as an optional account, community and synchronization provider.

history.event: Inferred from the technology-stack and platform-support requirements.

wish.title: Mobile web-first delivery
wish.description: Use an installable mobile website/PWA as the first smartphone client, with an optional native wrapper later when background execution, sensors or platform integration require it.

history.event: Inferred from the technology-stack and platform-support requirements.

wish.title: KISS-first architecture
wish.description: Prefer the simplest architecture that satisfies the current phase. Avoid duplicate frameworks, microservices, premature native clients, WebRTC, distributed infrastructure and extra languages until a concrete requirement justifies them.

history.event: Added after the user explicitly required the KISS principle.

wish.title: Browser-first with thin headless desktop companion
wish.description: Keep the tease player and primary interface in the normal browser. The installed desktop component should initially be a small headless CLI/native helper without its own full GUI. A browser extension may act as a narrow bridge where required, but the architecture must account for extension-store and browser-distribution constraints.

history.event: Added after clarifying that a full Electron/Tauri desktop user interface was not the intended baseline.

wish.title: Progressive TeaseScript language
wish.description: Create one progressively disclosed TeaseScript language and one deterministic runtime. Beginners use scenes and high-level commands; advanced authors can use typed state, functions, modules, records, collections, events and reusable libraries without needing unrestricted host-language code.

history.event: Added after comparing the uploaded SexScript Groovy and Tease AI Java/Spicy script systems.
Clarified that the goal is one runtime with beginner and advanced authoring levels, not multiple compatibility engines.

wish.title: Block syntax without significant whitespace
wish.description: Use explicit block delimiters such as braces rather than making indentation or spaces part of the script meaning.

history.event: Added after comparing the uploaded SexScript Groovy and Tease AI Java/Spicy script systems.

wish.title: Legacy compatibility without legacy semantics
wish.description: Support import or a compatibility parser for classic Tease AI scripts while keeping the new language grammar and semantics clean, versioned and unambiguous.

history.event: Added after comparing the uploaded SexScript Groovy and Tease AI Java/Spicy script systems.

wish.title: Sandboxed code modules and trusted capability plugins
wish.description: Allow advanced packages to include sandboxed TypeScript-derived libraries for complex algorithms and custom player interfaces. Libraries may use HTML, CSS and DOM inside the isolated player iframe, but receive no parent-site DOM, raw external network, filesystem, process or device objects. Separately enabled host integrations may expose controlled external features.

history.event: Added after comparing the uploaded SexScript Groovy and Tease AI Java/Spicy script systems.
Separated author-supplied sandboxed computation libraries from separately installed trusted engine plugins.
Removed unnecessary granular permissions; core engine APIs are standard and only genuine external boundaries require one-time enablement.
Allowed HTML/CSS/DOM inside the isolated player iframe while retaining the parent-site, network, filesystem, process and device boundaries.

wish.title: Reusable author libraries without mandatory programming
wish.description: Support versioned reusable libraries and packages so advanced authors can create Domme3-style helper APIs, while ordinary authors can create complete teases using only built-in high-level commands.

history.event: Added after reviewing the SexScripts API documentation and the Milovana EOS init-script pattern.

wish.title: Randomized module pools and session composition
wish.description: Support Tease AI-style reusable module pools so a main tease can assemble sessions dynamically from tagged or named modules, with weights, requirements, history avoidance, cooldowns and explicit fallback behavior. The composition logic must be visible in the script rather than hidden in hard-coded folder semantics.

history.event: Added after clarifying that randomized reusable file/module selection is a core benefit of classic Tease AI.

wish.title: Folder- and tag-based module selection
wish.description: Allow session builders to select random eligible `.tease` modules from a named folder or from the package-wide module index using required and excluded tags. Modules may carry multiple tags, with weights, conditions, cooldowns, recent-history avoidance and explicit fallback behavior.

history.event: Added while consolidating the ChatGPT project documentation and new reference examples.

wish.title: Continuous personality runtime
wish.description: Support CyberDom/VirMst-style personalities that remain active across a day or longer, with persistent events, schedules, statuses, jobs, deadlines, reminders, notifications, reports, permission requests, calendars and deterministic restart/resume behavior.

history.event: Added while consolidating the ChatGPT project documentation and new reference examples.

wish.title: Integrated browser code editor
wish.description: Provide a browser-based project editor with syntax highlighting, autocomplete, diagnostics, project tree, module/tag navigation, formatter, simulator, debugger, deterministic random tracing and publication validation.

history.event: Added while consolidating the ChatGPT project documentation and new reference examples.

wish.title: Beginner-friendly expression subsystem
wish.description: Provide readable arithmetic and logical expressions inside TeaseScript without exposing free JavaScript or TypeScript. Evaluate math.js as a restricted expression engine while retaining a separate parser for TeaseScript statements and blocks.

history.event: Added while consolidating the ChatGPT project documentation and new reference examples.
TeaseScript uses its own accepted expression grammar. Math.js remains only a proposed restricted evaluator and is not an accepted dependency.

wish.title: Capability parity from reference systems
wish.description: Use Milovana EOS, classic Tease AI, SexScripts/Groovy, Toy, Tease AI Java/Spicy and VirMst/CyberDom as capability references. The new engine should be able to recreate their useful behaviors through TeaseScript, libraries and capabilities without running their original languages or copying their architectures.

history.event: Added while consolidating the ChatGPT project documentation and new reference examples.

wish.title: Persistent personality instance
wish.description: Represent every long-running personality as a persistent server-side instance with status, timezone, revision, events, assignments, permissions, flags, reports and pending interactions.

history.event: Added after detailed analysis of Toy Groovy, VirMst and CyberDom capabilities.

wish.title: First-class lifecycle and scheduled events
wish.description: Support lifecycle events, explicit execution locations, missed-event policies and server scheduling while browsers are closed.

history.event: Added after detailed analysis of Toy Groovy, VirMst and CyberDom capabilities.

wish.title: Standard flags, assignments, permissions and reports
wish.description: Provide standardized runtime and API concepts for expiring flags, long-running assignments, permission requests and multi-question reports.

history.event: Added after detailed analysis of Toy Groovy, VirMst and CyberDom capabilities.

wish.title: Low-friction TypeScript library publication
wish.description: Use normal TypeScript exports as the public library API and generate manifests and editor metadata automatically. Built-in engine functions, events and planning require no permissions or duplicate registration; only external integrations are enabled at package or user level.

history.event: Added after detailed analysis of Toy Groovy, VirMst and CyberDom capabilities.
Removed unnecessary granular permissions; core engine APIs are standard and only genuine external boundaries require one-time enablement.

wish.title: Continuous-personality editor simulation
wish.description: Add timelines, calendars, status graphs, offline-time simulation, pending-event inspection and interrupt/resource tracing to the browser editor.

history.event: Added after detailed analysis of Toy Groovy, VirMst and CyberDom capabilities.

wish.title: Controlled interruption and event limits
wish.description: Use explicit procedure priority, interruption rules, resource claims, queue limits, deduplication and recurrence quotas for long-running personalities.

history.event: Added after detailed analysis of Toy Groovy, VirMst and CyberDom capabilities.

wish.title: Named multi-speaker chat messages
wish.description: Use the English `say` command without an implied Mistress argument. Allow authors to declare any practical number of speakers. Each speaker has only a unique identifier, display name, text color and font. Plain messages use quoted strings; backtick strings support `${expression}` interpolation; `say as <speakerIdentifier>` overrides the current default speaker for one message. Do not assume avatars, voices or other speaker properties.

history.event: Accepted as the preferred design for say and multi-speaker chat.
Restricted speaker properties to identifier, display name, color and font; removed unrequested avatar and voice assumptions.
Replaced the intermediate brace-message syntax with ADR 0011 quoted and backtick string literals.

wish.title: Cross-origin iframe player
wish.description: Run the complete player, engine and libraries in a sandboxed iframe on a separate player origin. Keep auth cookies host-only and pass only selected validated data through the parent bridge.

history.event: Confirmed during the iframe, library, text and concurrent-media design review.

wish.title: Full DOM custom interfaces
wish.description: Allow author libraries to use HTML, CSS and DOM inside the isolated player iframe, with optional Shadow DOM and no parent-site DOM access.

history.event: Confirmed during the iframe, library, text and concurrent-media design review.

wish.title: Standard and publishable author libraries
wish.description: Separate engine primitives, the official Standard Library and author libraries; allow reusable author-library publication.

history.event: Confirmed during the iframe, library, text and concurrent-media design review.

wish.title: Concurrent identified media playback
wish.description: Support blocking/background audio and video, simultaneous resources, durations, indefinite playback and per-resource handles for targeted stop/pause.

history.event: Confirmed during the iframe, library, text and concurrent-media design review.

wish.title: English implementation language
wish.description: Use English for source code, identifiers, TeaseScript keywords, APIs, comments, UI text and website content.

history.event: Confirmed during the iframe, library, text and concurrent-media design review.

wish.title: Tiered TypeScript strictness
wish.description: Use strict mode for engine and Standard Library; allow a friendlier private-author profile with explicit typed exports; require published libraries to compile fully strict.

history.event: Confirmed during the iframe, library, text and concurrent-media design review.

wish.title: Interpolated and formatted say text
wish.description: Use ordinary quoted strings for non-interpolated say text and backtick template strings for ${expression} interpolation. Both support explicit \n line breaks. Keep the switchable default speaker, Markdown plus limited BBCode, and raw HTML without compatibility guarantees.

history.event: Confirmed during the iframe, library, text and concurrent-media design review.
Selected quote strings for plain text and backtick templates for interpolation.

wish.title: Named custom views
wish.description: Use `set result = show custom view "view-id" with { ... }` for blocking views and `open custom view "view-id" as handle with { ... }`, `update handle with { ... }`, and `close handle` for background views. Transfer serializable data only.

history.event: Confirmed during the iframe, library, text and concurrent-media design review.
Selected blocking and background custom-view syntax with serializable input and results.
The custom-view capability remains desired, but the old exact syntax conflicted with accepted direct assignment and is no longer authoritative.

wish.title: Declared external hosts
wish.description: Require external API, media and widget hosts to be declared in package metadata.

history.event: Confirmed during the iframe, library, text and concurrent-media design review.
Replaced declared author-controlled external hosts with platform-hosted media and managed integrations.

wish.title: Platform-hosted media and managed external integrations
wish.description: Do not give package code unrestricted external network access. Upload package images, audio and video to platform storage/CDN, build widgets locally in author libraries, and add third-party APIs later as platform-managed Standard Library integrations.

history.event: Accepted after reviewing privacy, dead-link risk and long-term package reliability.

wish.title: Ordered unique set collection
wish.description: Support insertion-ordered scalar sets using set[...] and type set, with uniqueness, copy semantics, collection methods and no indexing.

history.event: Confirmed during the phase-1 core language and runtime design.
ADR 0013 accepted insertion-ordered scalar sets with set[...] literals and type set annotations.

wish.title: Deep value-copy semantics
wish.description: Assignments and declarations deep-copy ordinary lists, sets and script objects so mutations do not unexpectedly affect the source value.

history.event: Confirmed during the phase-1 core language and runtime design.
ADR 0014 accepted recursive value-copy semantics for ordinary script lists, sets, and objects.

wish.title: Serializable deterministic execution
wish.description: Compile AST to a versioned JSON-safe instruction plan and execute with explicit JSON-safe runtime snapshots and checkpoints rather than a suspended JavaScript call stack.

history.event: Confirmed during the phase-1 core language and runtime design.
The core interpreter is merged on main; serializable instruction plans, runtime snapshots, and checkpoints are the next in-progress milestone and are not yet in the main baseline.

ADR 0015 is accepted. A versioned serializable instruction plan, runtime snapshot, sequenced events, deterministic RNG state, and self-contained checkpoints are implemented on PR #3 at b9cb50a07e42b31ba79a5a7d26d6c172de33b533; merge and hardening remain.

The serializable runtime/playground and explicit control-flow frames are present in the recorded merged main baseline 348170dd75598a1265f82e22a1f0e3cd8a639321. Serializable user-function frames and checkpoint/evaluation hardening were committed on feature/functions-runtime in df51ebe.

wish.title: Standalone browser playground
wish.description: Provide a development playground that loads a repository main.tease example and exposes diagnostics, instructions, runtime state, events, run, step, reset and checkpoint controls.

history.event: Confirmed during the phase-1 core language and runtime design.
The standalone browser playground is being implemented on feature/runtime-playground and is not yet part of the main baseline.
The standalone repository-backed browser playground and dependency-free development server are implemented on PR #3; the branch reported 127 passing tests and an HTTP smoke test.
The standalone browser playground is present in the recorded merged main baseline 348170dd75598a1265f82e22a1f0e3cd8a639321 and now includes fixed control-flow, checkpoint-loop, and function examples.

wish.title: Custom-view capability with syntax pending
wish.description: Support blocking and background custom views inside the sandboxed player iframe using serializable boundary data; finalize exact TeaseScript syntax later.

history.event: Confirmed during the phase-1 core language and runtime design.
The custom-view capability remains accepted while exact author syntax and lifecycle semantics remain open.

technology_stack_proposal.authority: Non-authoritative historical planning snapshot. Use CURRENT-DESIGN.md, current specifications and accepted ADRs.

component.name: server
component.purpose: One server application for accounts, forum, scripts, progress, moderation and API.

component.name: database
component.purpose: Single relational database for all central platform data.

component.name: web_client
component.purpose: Responsive web interface and browser tease player without a separate frontend server.

component.name: mobile
component.purpose: Android and iPhone access through the same website; no native app initially.

component.name: realtime
component.technology: HTTPS first; WebSocket only where needed
component.purpose: Do not introduce WebRTC until cross-device direct communication is a real requirement.

component.name: deployment
component.purpose: Run one application and one PostgreSQL database; use Caddy or Nginx and avoid microservices.

component.name: media
component.technology: Platform-managed object storage/CDN + optional private local media
component.purpose: Keep published packages reproducible and independent of dead hotlinks while allowing private user-owned media to remain local.

phase.title: Browser MVP
phase.scope: Accounts, simple forum, script upload, deterministic browser engine, local media import and progress storage.

phase.title: Community platform
phase.scope: Editor, publishing workflow, moderation, synchronization and optional server previews.

phase.title: Desktop companion
phase.scope: Tauri client for local LLM, toys, FFmpeg, local folders and OS-specific tasks.

phase.title: Cross-device features
phase.scope: Phone sensors, device handoff and WebRTC only after the basic platform is stable.

component.name: desktop_primary
component.purpose: A small installed command-line/native host with no mandatory GUI; it provides local files, process launch, Ollama, Intiface and selected OS capabilities.

component.name: browser_bridge
component.purpose: A deliberately thin browser-specific relay between the website and native companion. Do not place tease-engine business logic in the extension.

component.name: desktop_optional_later
component.technology: Tauri only when a standalone desktop experience is justified
component.purpose: Reuse the web interface in a system webview without adopting Electron; the browser extension is then unnecessary inside the Tauri build.

notes.desktop_architecture_clarification: The browser remains the primary tease environment. The native companion is initially headless. Native Messaging is technically appropriate but must not become the only possible transport until distribution constraints are resolved.
notes.companion_language_decision: Go is the KISS candidate for the first thin companion; Rust remains the stronger later choice for embedded Buttplug/native integrations or deeper OS control.

script_language_proposal.authority: Non-authoritative historical planning snapshot. Use current TeaseScript/library specifications and accepted ADRs.
script_language_proposal.name: TeaseScript
script_language_proposal.syntax: Narrative-first line syntax with explicit brace-delimited blocks; indentation is cosmetic.
script_language_proposal.execution: Parsed and compiled by the shared TypeScript tease-core into a versioned intermediate representation; executed by a deterministic sandboxed runtime.
script_language_proposal.authoring_levels: Level 1 scenes and commands; level 2 state, conditions and reusable procedures; level 3 modules, events, collections and dynamic selection; trusted TypeScript plugins outside normal script packages.
script_language_proposal.compatibility: Classic Tease AI, SexScript Groovy and Tease AI Java scripts are migration sources or compatibility formats, not unrestricted execution formats for newly published scripts.
script_language_proposal.runtime_model: One engine and scheduler execute a versioned intermediate representation. TeaseScript compiles to that representation; sandboxed code modules may only return data, validated state patches or action plans.
script_language_proposal.advanced_code_model: Do not scatter inline eval blocks through pages. Advanced code belongs in named, versioned modules with explicit exports and resource limits.
script_language_proposal.state_mutation_model: Author code never receives a mutable reference to internal runtime state. The engine validates all state changes for type, ownership and authority before applying them.
script_language_proposal.milovana_inspiration: Retain EOS's useful combination of declarative pages and an init-code escape hatch, but replace global init/eval strings with first-class modules, typed APIs and controlled state patches.
script_language_proposal.entrypoint_model: Each package has one explicit main entry point. Main may call an introduction and then invoke a session builder.
script_language_proposal.module_model: Modules are first-class named units with metadata such as tags, weight, requirements, cooldown and history keys. A session builder can select and run modules randomly or according to rules. Physical folders are organizational only.
